babyDevelopment: Implantation complete! The blastocyst attaches to the uterine wall. The inner cells will become the embryo, while the outer cells form the placenta. This week, the amniotic sac and yolk sac also begin to develop.
bodyChanges: You may miss your period. Subtle signs like breast tenderness, fatigue, and a heightened sense of smell might begin. Hormonal shifts are now significant as hCG levels double every 48-72 hours.
tips: Take a home pregnancy test if your period is late
Inform your partner and share the joy!

do: Switch to a pregnancy-safe skincare routine
Eat smaller meals to manage early nausea
Listen to your body's need for rest

travelAdvisory.note: Safe to travel, but expect some fatigue.

warningSignsToWatch: Severe one-sided pelvic pain (ectopic risk indicator)
milestoneDescription: Implantation is successful. You are officially pregnant!
